# Phase 8 Epic — Post-Drain Hardening: platform debt, decomposition, deferred follow-ups

**Labels:** `phase-8`, `epic`
**Status:** Planned (generated by /triage-and-plan, 2026-06-11)

## Context

The autonomous backlog drain (issues #261–#429, PRs #410–#455) shipped the
cms-migration epic (#388), live BotMason providers (#401), the offline
check-in queue, the stuck-user recovery replay, and both halves of the local
Gate 2. Along the way it deliberately deferred a set of follow-ups — each
marked in code comments, PR review notes, or issue bodies — and left several
structural debts untouched. This phase collects them into atomized issues.

Audit dimensions and headline findings:

- **Architectural health:** `frontend/src/api/index.ts` is a 2,639-line god
  module that every feature edits; `GoalModal.tsx` (1,371 lines) is the
  largest component in the app.
- **Deferred contracts:** the `Page` envelope migration (#408) explicitly
  deferred three endpoint groups and gated the backend default-flip on them;
  the offline check-in queue (#415 / PR #450) shipped with two
  reviewer-flagged rough edges; dark-mode tokens (BUG-FE-UI-003) export a
  palette nothing consumes.
- **Test quality:** BotMason's streaming paths are `pragma: no cover`;
  `jest.config.js` documents two deferred test-infra fixes
  (BUG-FE-TEST-001 / BUG-FE-TEST-002).
- **Toolchain:** pre-commit pins ruff v0.12.9 / mypy v1.17.1 while the venv
  runs ruff 0.15.16 / mypy 2.1.0 — divergent rule sets already caused
  local-vs-hook lint mismatches during #423.
- **Content pipeline:** chapter Markdown renders natively but relative
  images are dropped (#394's deliberate stopgap) — the manifest's `media`
  field has no app-side consumer.

Total: 11 issues, ~2,675 LoC.

## Dependency graph

```
phase-8-01 (api split core)
  └── phase-8-02 (api split remainder + barrel)

phase-8-04 (envelope completion)
  └── phase-8-05 (paginate default flip)

phase-8-03, 06, 07, 08, 09, 10, 11 — independent, parallelizable
```

## Out of scope (tracked elsewhere)

- GitHub epics #336 (grounding techniques) and #345 (customizable practices)
  already have decomposed issue files (`grounding-techniques-*.md`,
  `custom-practices-*.md`) ready for /continue-epic.
- The httpOnly-cookie web session (`authStorage.ts`'s "post-MVP epic") —
  needs a product/infra decision first.
- #411 (dependabot review secrets) — blocked on an operator decision.
- Content-repo tasks (`cms-migration/content-A..E`) live in the
  aptitude-course repository.
